_paths_comment: All paths are portable: ${HOUFIN_DATA} (raw + processed 27 km products) and ${HOUFIN_PROCESSED} (model inputs/results) expand at load time (src/config_utils.py). They default to <repo>/data and <repo>/data/processed for local dev; on HPC export HOUFIN_DATA/HOUFIN_PROCESSED.

trends._comment: Gridded trend products aligned to the model grid by preprocess/bbs_trend.py and preprocess/ebird_trend.py (both keyed to community_trend_list). BBS: long-term %/yr per cell (nearest clip/pad, zero resampling -- ref grid is on the BBS lattice). eBird: abd_ppy (%/yr) + abd (mid-window rel. abundance) per cell, centroids projected from WGS84 and binned. Consumed by community_encoder/train_DESK/trend_community.py.

_ebird_trends_comment: eBird Status & Trends 'trends' product, fetched from the SAME REST API + key as the weekly abundance (acquire/ebird.py --trends), NOT the ebirdst R package. Trends live under version year 2022 (2023 has none); objkey {year}/{sp}/trends/{sp}_{season}_ebird-trends_{year}.parquet, the season token discovered per-species from the listing. Tabular (one row per 27 km cell): abd_ppy (%/yr trend), abd_trend (cumulative %), abd (mid-window relative abundance), WGS84 lon/lat centroids, window 2012-2022.
_community_trend_comment: Written by identify/select_trend_community.py: the top-N (bbs.community_top_n) House-Finch-similar species present in BOTH trend products (BBS tr{AOU}.tif AND eBird has-trends), House Finch excluded. Columns species_code, aou, season, sci_norm, mean_rank. Drives the trend downloads + the trend->annual community cube. Differs from the weekly community.
_focal_comment: The transfer TARGET of the downstream statistical model. avonet.py already excludes it from reference_community_ranked.csv; this is the defense-in-depth code that the eBird selection + BBS crosswalk also filter, so a stale ranked list can never leak House Finch into the community Z.

timeline._comment: Single source of truth for the model timeline (see src/temporal.py, docs/TEMPORAL.md). Model year T = climate bio-year Aug(T-1)->Jul(T), aligned to the ~June BBS count. first_year=1902 (first FULL bio-year, since climate obs start Jan 1901); end_year=2025 (newest BBS: 2026 release, 1966-2025 field seasons); invasion_year=1940 (NYC release; inv_timestep is DERIVED as invasion_year-first_year, never hardcoded). Land use/soil use the calendar-year-T state; only climate uses the bio-year window. Lagging covariates (e.g. LUH-3 ends 2024) are EMA-carried to end_year.

grid._box_comment: Fixed study-area extent (left, bottom, right, top) in box_crs = USA Contiguous Albers NAD83 (ESRI:102003), the native CRS of the USGS BBS mapping rasters (trend/abundance, 27 km). The box is the lon/lat region [-125..-66, 24..53] (CONUS + southern Canada; AK, far-northern Canada and most of Mexico clipped out) reprojected to 102003, with its ORIGIN SNAPPED to the BBS 27 km lattice (residues x=20068.8961, y=15815.4047 mod 27000) so BBS trend rasters land on this grid with ZERO resampling (clip/pad only). 133 rows x 224 cols at 27 km. build_ref_grid defaults to these when given no --base-raster/--bounds, so the canonical ref grid builds standalone on any machine.
grid._comment: Single source of truth for the model grid. target_res_m is THE model resolution (27 km), matching the native resolution of the trend products this pipeline targets (USGS BBS mapping rasters, 27 km ESRI:102003; eBird Status & Trends, 27 km). Continental products (climate/climr, LUH-3, HYDE, SoilGrids, DEM, eBird abundance) are aggregated onto ref_raster via regrid.reproject_to_ref `average`; the BBS trend rasters share this CRS+lattice and align by nearest clip/pad (no resampling of the ground truth). Nonlinear steps (Ruzicka kernel/PCA for Z, quantiles, transforms) are applied at this resolution -- Z is built at 27 km, never pooled. The prior 25 km ESRI:102039 CONUS box lives in git history; the older 16 km / 4 km BUI-grid pipeline is preserved under src/data/deprecated/.

coastline._comment: Terrestrial mask (src/data/preprocess/land_mask.py). Natural Earth 10m polygonal lakes are subtracted from the continental land layer on a fine grid before aggregation, so Great Lakes are water while narrow rivers are never expanded. The terrestrial fraction is thresholded at land_fraction_tau (>=tau => model land). This is water masking only; out-of-scope terrestrial regions need a separate study-domain mask.

avonet._comment: Reference-community identification inputs, all auto-fetched by acquire/avonet.py. AVONET traits + BirdLife/BirdTree crosswalk + precomputed Hackett MCC phylogeny (.nex) from figshare ELEData.zip (found by name via the figshare v2 API) -> {datasets_root}/avonet. eBird taxonomy from the eBird API (ref/taxonomy/ebird, no key; the Cornell web CSV is Cloudflare-walled). Urban-tolerance indices from a separate public figshare article -> {datasets_root}/urban_avian.

dem._comment: Global DEM for the climate elevation-quantile step (preprocess/elevation.py -> climr). Default: NOAA NCEI ETOPO 2022 60-arc-second (~1.85 km) surface elevation, a single global GeoTIFF over public HTTPS (no auth). Swap `url` for a finer DEM (e.g. GMTED2010 7.5-arc-second) if desired; elevation.py is source-agnostic.

elevation._comment: preprocess/elevation.py reprojects the DEM onto a sub-grid fine_factor x finer than the model grid, then takes per-cell p10/p50/p90 elevation. fine_factor should be >= target_res/DEM_native so no DEM detail is lost (27 km / ~1.85 km ETOPO ~= 14.6 -> 15).

climate._comment: climr downscaling. mode 'subgrid' (default): a grid x grid mesh of true-elevation sub-points per cell -> real spatial+elevation quantiles (captures horizontal gradients). mode 'elev_quantile': centroid at three elevation quantiles (elevation-only, cheaper). obs_ts_dataset feeds the annual observed series; obs year range clamps to climr's extent.

luh3._comment: LUH-3 v1.2 CMIP7 Historical (850-2024, 0.25deg). states + management only; transitions (17GB, flows not states) skipped.
luh3._exclude_comment: Uninformative management layers dropped from the covariate set: forest plantations (pltns, all-zero in LUH-3 V1), additional tree cover (addtc), 1st/2nd-generation biofuel crop fractions (cpbf1_*, cpbf2_*), plantation biofuel (pltns_bfuel), and wood-harvest carbon split (rndwd/fulwd/combf). All ~0 over North America.

hyde._comment: HYDE 3.5 baseline netCDF (5 arc-min global, one file per variable spanning all time points). Confirmed accessible over plain HTTP at geo.public.data.uu.nl (not the bot-protected public.yoda.uu.nl). Add urban_area.nc etc. to files if needed.

sciencebase._comment: USGS BBS releases via ScienceBase item file API. bbs = newest US/Canada release (2026 Release, 1966-2025). bbs_mexico = 2008-2018 UNPROCESSED (DOI 10.5066/P9L4KBDC); lacks RunType/RPID, incorporated with a quality covariate.

bbs_mexico._comment: whole_item (archive), NOT per-file. SpeciesData.csv is BROKEN upstream on ScienceBase: the record claims 1.6 MB + an MD5, but the per-file downloadUri 404s and every archive ships it as 0 bytes (verified 2026-07). Per-file download therefore exits nonzero on the 404 and would abort download_all.sh; the whole-item archive downloads cleanly (exit 0) with an empty SpeciesData, so the ingest just skips Mexico (non-fatal). Drop a valid SpeciesData.csv in bbs_mexico_unprocessed/ to enable Mexico if you obtain one elsewhere.

bbs_trends._comment: USGS BBS Mapping products 1966-2022 (DOI 10.5066/P1DPJPSI): per-species trend rasters tr{AOU}.tif = long-term geometric-mean %/yr population change 1966-2022, 27 km ESRI:102003 (NAD83 Albers), 516 species, 5-digit AOU code in the filename. Run download with --extract to unpack tr*.tif.

bbs_abundance._comment: Same ScienceBase item as bbs_trends: the ABUNDANCE rasters ra{AOU}.tif = 2018-2022 mean relative abundance (birds/route), 27 km ESRI:102003, 520 species. Used ONLY as the method-B deep-reconstruction scale (trend_community: deep past = k*B/f, k=median(eBird/BBS present)), NOT as community abundance values. Run download with --extract to unpack ra*.tif.
